title: Fixing Broken HTML List Tags with Invalid 'type' Attributes

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate a given HTML list tag. The function should check if the 'type' attribute is valid. A valid 'type' attribute is either '1' or '2'. If the 'type' attribute is invalid, return an error message. Otherwise, return the valid HTML list tag.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the HTML list tag.
statement.outputFormat: A string representing the valid HTML list tag, or an error message if the 'type' attribute is invalid.
statement.notes: The function should not return any HTML tags without a valid 'type' attribute.

constraints.description: The length of the input string
